Title: Complete biosynthetic pathway of furochromones in Saposhnikovia divaricata and its evolutionary mechanism in Apiaceae plants

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts malonyl-CoA to noreugenin, forming the chromone skeleton.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as likely initial/rate-limiting control step by evolutionary/expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in roots; expression highly correlated with furochromone contents
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in S. divaricata roots; transiently active in N. benthamiana leaves after agroinfiltration
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of furochromone pathway products by providing noreugenin precursor; high root expression contributes to abundant furochromones in S. divaricata.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: coexpression analysis; recombinant expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay; HPLC/LC-MS; kinetic analysis; transient pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana; comparative genomics/transcriptomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key upstream enzyme for pathway engineering; should be included in heterologous reconstruction/stacking to boost furochromone production, and high-expression alleles are valuable for metabolic engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SdPCS is proximal-duplication derived from nearby SdPCS2 and shows much higher expression than Apiaceous homologs; authors infer this high expression profoundly contributes to root furochromone accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SdPCS catalyzes formation of noreugenin from malonyl-CoA. 2. Its expression is strongly correlated with furochromone accumulation in roots. 3. Comparative analysis indicates proximal duplication and exceptionally high expression of SdPCS are major evolutionary drivers of abundant furochromones in S. divaricata.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a dimethylallyl group to C-6 of noreugenin to form peucenin.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; low catalytic efficiency noted in heterologous reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expression highly correlated with furochromone contents
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: active in agroinfiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables prenylated intermediate formation needed for downstream furochromone biosynthesis; low catalytic efficiency limited heterologous yield.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: candidate screening by coexpression; yeast microsome expression; in vitro enzyme assay with DMAPP; HPLC/LC-MS; NMR structural confirmation of product; kinetic analysis; transient pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential pathway enzyme for de novo furochromone production; enzyme optimization or stronger expression may improve engineering yield because low activity constrained production.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low catalytic efficiency in N. benthamiana required increased Agrobacterium OD for better product yield.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First reported prenyltransferase utilizing chromones as substrate. SdPT2 also has same function.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SdPT catalyzes C-6 prenylation of noreugenin to peucenin. 2. Product identity was confirmed by LC-MS/MS and NMR. 3. SdPT is essential but relatively inefficient in heterologous furochromone reconstruction.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative cyclization of the prenyl side chain of peucenin to generate the fused dihydrofuran ring of visamminol.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key lineage-specific control point for furochromone formation in Apiaceae
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expression highly correlated with furochromone contents; present in S. divaricata
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: active in agroinfiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables formation of the furochromone skeleton and its lineage-specific presence explains abundant furochromone accumulation in S. divaricata compared with other Apiaceae species.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: coexpression-guided candidate selection; yeast microsome assays; LC-MS; product comparison with hydrolyzed standard and NMR; comparative genomics/synteny/phylogeny; transient pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical enzyme to include for engineering true furochromone production; lineage-specific presence makes it a key transfer target into non-producing hosts or low-producing Apiaceae.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among tested Apiaceous homologs, only SdPC effectively produced visamminol. It is a novel homologous enzyme with distinct function from related furocoumarin cyclases.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SdPC catalyzes oxidative cyclization of peucenin to visamminol. 2. It is the lineage-specific missing step explaining high furochromone accumulation in S. divaricata. 3. Most homologs from other Apiaceae lacked effective activity in vitro.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates C-11 of visamminol and 5-O-methylvisamminol to produce norcimifugin and cimifugin, respectively.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expression correlated with total contents of cimifugin and POG
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: active in agroinfiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces C-11 hydroxylation required for formation of cimifugin/POG branch metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transcriptome-guided candidate screening; yeast microsome assays; LC-MS; transient pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Needed to access the cimifugin/POG branch in metabolic engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalyzes hydroxylation on both unmethylated and 5-O-methylated intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SdCH converts visamminol to norcimifugin and 5-O-methylvisamminol to cimifugin. 2. It defines the hydroxylated branch leading to POG. 3. Activity was validated in yeast microsomes and in Nicotiana transient expression.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates the 5-hydroxyl group of visamminol and norcimifugin to generate 5-O-methylvisamminol and cimifugin, respectively.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: active in agroinfiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: SAM and DTT required in assay
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates methylated furochromone intermediates required for 5-O-MVG and cimifugin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: candidate selection by coexpression; recombinant expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay; LC-MS; transient pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required for engineering the 5-O-methylated branch and synthesis of 5-O-MVG/cimifugin-derived products.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalyzes methylation on both pre- and post-hydroxylation intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SdOMT methylates visamminol and norcimifugin at 5-OH. 2. It produces 5-O-methylvisamminol and cimifugin. 3. This enzyme is part of the reconstructed full furochromone pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glucosylates the 3'-OH of 5-O-methylvisamminol and the 11-OH of cimifugin to form 5-O-MVG and POG, respectively.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: root-expressed candidate UGT; not as strongly correlated with organ distribution as most upstream genes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: active in agroinfiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly forms the major glycosylated end products 5-O-MVG and POG.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assays; LC-MS/MS comparison with standards; kinetic analysis; transient pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value terminal enzyme for producing the major medicinal glycosides; preferable for engineering because of higher catalytic efficiency than SdUGT2.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Preferentially catalyzes 11-O-glycosylation of cimifugin; higher catalytic efficiency than SdUGT2 and especially strong preference for substrate 8 over 5.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SdUGT1 directly synthesizes 5-O-MVG and POG from 5 and 8, respectively. 2. It strongly prefers cimifugin as substrate. 3. It is the more efficient terminal glycosyltransferase among the two identified UGTs.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UGT93 family glycosyltransferase; UGT93BB1; GT-B fold
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glucosylates the 3'-OH of 5-O-methylvisamminol and the 11-OH of cimifugin to produce 5-O-MVG and POG.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: root-expressed candidate UGT; not as strongly correlated with organ distribution as most upstream genes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: active in agroinfiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly forms terminal glycosylated furochromones, with preference for 11-O-glycosylation of cimifugin over 3'-O-glycosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assays; LC-MS/MS comparison with standards; kinetic analysis; X-ray crystallography (PDB 8ZNK); docking; alanine scanning; transient pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful terminal glycosyltransferase for pathway engineering; structure-guided engineering may further tune regioselectivity and catalytic efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower catalytic efficiency than SdUGT1 for final product formation in some contexts.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Crystal structure revealed GT-B fold. Structural modeling indicates preference for 11-O-glycosylation because substrate 8 forms more favorable interactions in that mode; no further glycosylation of 9 observed.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SdUGT2 catalyzes terminal glucosylation to form 5-O-MVG and POG. 2. Structural analysis explains its preference for 11-O-glycosylation of cimifugin. 3. SdUGT2 is functionally redundant with but less efficient than SdUGT1.
